sections.paragraph:
[in Thes פֵּשֶׁת] with suffix פִּשְׁתִּי Hos 2:7, 11 and פִּשְׁתָּה plur. פִּשְׁתִּים fem. ( Isa 19:9)-

(1) flax, sing., Exo 9:31 plur., Levit. 13:47 , seq.; Deu 22:11 Isa 19:9 Jerem. 13:1 . פִּשְׁתֵּי הָעֵץ Jos 2:6, flax of tree, cotton (see the root), (both flax and cotton are also expressed in Syriac and Arabic by the same word, ܟܶܬܳܢܐܳ, ىُتُنُ); but this is rendered by LXX., Vulg., Syr., stalks of flax, prop. flax of wood, or flax wood, which would rather be called עֲצֵי הַפִּשְׁתִּים.

(2) a wick made of flax or cotton, Isa 42:3 43:17 . (Foster, De Bysso Antiqu., p. 63, considers פִּשְׁתֶּה to be of Egyptian origin, from ϣⲉⲁⲧⲥⲓ, prop. thread plant, with the art, pi.).
